wholeFileCssHtmlNodesTypedAsFunction.description: CSS and HTML file-level nodes had id prefix 'function:' but type='function'. Fixed to type='file' because these represent whole files, not functions. Affected: frontend/admin/menus/*.{css,html}, frontend/admin/orders/*.{css,html}, frontend/auth/*.{css,html}, frontend/menus/*.{css,html}, frontend/orders/*.{css,html}, legacy-step2-backup/admin/menus/*.{css,html}

wholeFileJsNodesTypedAsFunction.description: JS file-level nodes (where id path segment equals the full file path, e.g. function:frontend/menus/detail.js:frontend/menus/detail.js) had type='function'. Fixed to type='file'. Affected: frontend/menus/detail.js, frontend/menus/list.js, frontend/my/index.{css,html,js}, frontend/orders/detail.js, frontend/orders/list.js, legacy-step2-backup/admin/menus/create.js, detail.js, edit.js, list.js, legacy-step2-backup/js/data.js, README.md

fileIdNodesWithWrongType.description: Nodes with 'file:' prefix but type was 'config' or 'document'. Normalized to 'file'. Affected: file:.understand-anything/config.json (was 'config'), file:backend/README.md (was 'document'), file:BLUEPRINT.md (was 'document')

definedInEdges.description: 26 edges had type='contains' but label='defined in' with source=child-function, target=parent-file. This is semantically inverted for 'contains' (which should go parent→child). Changed edge type to 'defined_in' to correctly express the relationship direction.

droppedEdges.decision: Acceptable — these were internal 'calls' edges targeting minor helper functions (under 10-line significance threshold) that were not extracted as nodes. No stub nodes created per instructions.

remainingConcerns: Some 'function:' prefix nodes have type='file' (e.g. function:frontend/admin/menus/create.css:create.css). The ID prefix 'function:' is inconsistent with type='file'. Ideally these would use 'file:' prefix IDs, but changing IDs would require updating all edge references. The type field is now correct; the prefix mismatch is a cosmetic issue that downstream graph consumers should handle by checking the 'type' field, not the prefix.
The 'contains' edges from batches 7-8 where source=file and target=function (lines 3129-3350) correctly model containment parent→child, so those are fine.

13 nodes in batch 7-part-2 and batch 8 have 'defined_in' edges pointing to whole-file nodes that use the 'function:' id prefix (e.g. function:frontend/menus/detail.js:frontend/menus/detail.js). These parent nodes are now correctly typed as 'file', but the edge structure (child function → parent file-level node via defined_in) is semantically sound.
